title: What should a separate visual debugger frontend become?
summary: A custom debugger with tensor-network, mesh, graphics, and richer state visualization is deliberately deferred because its interaction model needs sustained owner-in-the-loop design and may belong outside Neovim.
details: The existing DAP usability is not treated as an adequate design baseline for extension.
Tensor-network views, graphics/mesh rendering, and graphics-debugger integration require flexible non-text panels and coordinated state inspection.
Neovim may remain an editor/control surface, but a separate native or browser application is an open architectural option.
The function probe is not a debugger substitute: it compiles explicit invocations and returns process output, not arbitrary paused-program state.
No debugger implementation work is authorized by the current tooling pass; a future design round should begin with concrete workflows and visual prototypes.

provenance.note: Owner explicitly benched debugger work because it requires more direct and subtle interaction.

questions: Why was a new debugger not implemented in this pass?
Should the future debugger live inside Neovim?

last_verified.method: Direct owner scope decision; intentionally left open.
